title: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Rise of Digital Nomads
rawPassageText:
READING PASSAGE 1
You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 1-13, which are based on Reading Passage 1 below.

The Rise of Digital Nomads

A

A digital nomad is usually defined as someone who uses digital technologies to work remotely while living or travelling away from a fixed workplace. The idea is not completely new: freelance writers, consultants and programmers were working from laptops long before the term became fashionable. What changed after the pandemic was the scale of remote work and the number of people who realised that a job did not always have to be tied to one city. By 2026, digital nomads are no longer viewed only as adventurous backpackers with blogs. Many are salaried employees, business owners or independent contractors who carry out ordinary professional tasks from temporary homes abroad.

B

Governments have responded by creating special visa schemes for remote workers. These visas are intended to regularise a practice that had often existed in a legal grey area, because many people worked online while entering countries as tourists. Most schemes require applicants to show proof of income, health insurance and a contract with a foreign employer or clients outside the host country. The logic is simple: if digital nomads earn money elsewhere but spend it locally, they may support restaurants, transport services, language schools and short-term accommodation without directly competing for local jobs.

C

The benefits are not evenly distributed. Cities with beaches, reliable internet and relatively low living costs can attract a sudden flow of higher-earning visitors. Cafes, coworking spaces and apartment owners may welcome the spending, but residents may see rents rise faster than wages. Some critics argue that digital nomads can unintentionally reshape neighbourhoods by increasing demand for furnished flats and international services. Others point out that many nomads stay for only a few months, so their connection with local communities may remain shallow even when their economic impact is visible.

D

Employers face a different problem: compliance. If an employee works from another country without permission, the company may create tax, immigration or employment-law obligations without realising it. A worker who spends several months abroad may also handle client data across borders, use unsecured networks or fall outside the insurance arrangements that normally apply at home. For this reason, some firms have introduced formal work-from-anywhere policies. These often limit the number of days employees can spend overseas and require approval before travel begins.

E

Digital nomads themselves also describe a mixture of freedom and pressure. They may enjoy choosing where to live, learning languages and avoiding long commutes. However, the lifestyle can involve loneliness, time-zone problems and constant administration. Opening bank accounts, renewing visas and finding quiet places for video meetings can take more energy than social media suggests. Experienced nomads often say that success depends less on travel inspiration than on routine: stable income, careful planning and the ability to work when a new city is more tempting than a laptop screen.

F

The rise of digital nomads therefore raises a broader question about the future of work. If jobs can move across borders more easily than people, governments must decide how to tax, welcome and regulate mobile workers. Host cities must also balance visitor income against housing pressure and community change. The digital nomad is sometimes presented as a symbol of personal freedom, but the phenomenon is also a test of institutions designed for a world in which work, residence and citizenship usually belonged in the same place.

Questions 1-7

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 1?

In boxes 1-7 on your answer sheet, write

YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

1 Digital nomads existed before the expression became widely used.
2 Digital nomads are more likely than ordinary tourists to travel with children.
3 Digital nomad visas usually provide a direct route to citizenship.
4 Most digital nomad visas are designed for people who work for employers or clients in the host country.
5 Some governments believe digital nomads can bring foreign income into the local economy without taking local jobs.
6 In every host city, digital nomads earn more than local residents.
7 Companies may face legal or tax problems if employees work abroad without approval.

Questions 8-13

Complete the summary below.

Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.

Write your answers in boxes 8-13 on your answer sheet.

Digital nomads and host countries

Digital nomads use digital 8 .................... to work remotely while moving between places. Many countries have created special 9 .................... to make this form of work more legally visible. Applicants are often asked to provide evidence of their 10 .................... and health 11 .................... . Host economies may benefit from nomads' local 12 ...................., although residents sometimes worry that housing 13 .................... will increase.
rawAnswerKey:
Question 1: Digital nomads existed before the expression became widely used.

Correct Answer: YES

Accepted Answers: YES

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Rise of Digital Nomads

Exact Portion: The idea is not completely new: freelance writers, consultants and programmers were working from laptops long before the term became fashionable.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ YES เพราะข้อความในโจทย์สอดคล้องกับหลักฐานใน passage: “The idea is not completely new: freelance writers, consultants and programmers were working from laptops long before the term became fashionable.” ข้อบอกว่า digital nomads มีอยู่ก่อนคำนี้แพร่หลาย ซึ่งตรงกับ long before the term became fashionable ประเด็นสำคัญคือคำในโจทย์ถูก paraphrase เป็นคำใน passage แต่ความหมายยังเหมือนเดิม จึงเลือก YES

Paraphrased Vocabulary: existed before = were working long before; expression became widely used = term became fashionable; answer signal = YES

Question 2: Digital nomads are more likely than ordinary tourists to travel with children.

Correct Answer: NOT GIVEN

Accepted Answers: NOT GIVEN

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Rise of Digital Nomads

Exact Portion: Many are salaried employees, business owners or independent contractors who carry out ordinary professional tasks from temporary homes abroad.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ NOT GIVEN เพราะ passage มีข้อมูลใกล้เคียงว่า “Many are salaried employees, business owners or independent contractors who carry out ordinary professional tasks from temporary homes abroad.” แต่ไม่ได้ยืนยันรายละเอียดเฉพาะที่โจทย์ถาม passage ไม่ได้ให้ข้อมูลเรื่อง travelling with children หรือเปรียบเทียบกับ ordinary tourists ดังนั้นไม่มีหลักฐานพอจะตอบ YES/NO หรือ TRUE/FALSE จึงเป็น NOT GIVEN

Paraphrased Vocabulary: more likely = no comparison; travel with children = no information; answer signal = NOT GIVEN

Question 3: Digital nomad visas usually provide a direct route to citizenship.

Correct Answer: NOT GIVEN

Accepted Answers: NOT GIVEN

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Rise of Digital Nomads

Exact Portion: Governments have responded by creating special visa schemes for remote workers.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ NOT GIVEN เพราะ passage มีข้อมูลใกล้เคียงว่า “Governments have responded by creating special visa schemes for remote workers.” แต่ไม่ได้ยืนยันรายละเอียดเฉพาะที่โจทย์ถาม passage กล่าวถึง special visa schemes แต่ไม่ได้บอกว่า usually provide a direct route to citizenship หรือไม่ ดังนั้นไม่มีหลักฐานพอจะตอบ YES/NO หรือ TRUE/FALSE จึงเป็น NOT GIVEN

Paraphrased Vocabulary: digital nomad visas = special visa schemes; citizenship = no information; answer signal = NOT GIVEN

Question 4: Most digital nomad visas are designed for people who work for employers or clients in the host country.

Correct Answer: NO

Accepted Answers: NO

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Rise of Digital Nomads

Exact Portion: Most schemes require applicants to show proof of income, health insurance and a contract with a foreign employer or clients outside the host country.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ NO เพราะข้อความในโจทย์ขัดกับหลักฐานใน passage: “Most schemes require applicants to show proof of income, health insurance and a contract with a foreign employer or clients outside the host country.” ข้อบอกว่า work for employers/clients in the host country แต่ passage บอกว่า foreign employer หรือ clients outside the host country จึงขัดกัน จุดตัดสินคือ passage ให้ข้อมูลตรงข้ามกับโจทย์ ไม่ใช่แค่ให้รายละเอียดเพิ่ม จึงต้องตอบ NO

Paraphrased Vocabulary: designed for = require applicants; in the host country = outside the host country; answer signal = NO

Question 5: Some governments believe digital nomads can bring foreign income into the local economy without taking local jobs.

Correct Answer: YES

Accepted Answers: YES

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Rise of Digital Nomads

Exact Portion: if digital nomads earn money elsewhere but spend it locally, they may support restaurants, transport services, language schools and short-term accommodation without directly competing for local jobs.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ YES เพราะข้อความในโจทย์สอดคล้องกับหลักฐานใน passage: “if digital nomads earn money elsewhere but spend it locally, they may support restaurants, transport services, language schools and short-term accommodation without directly competing for local jobs.” bring foreign income = earn money elsewhere but spend locally และ without taking local jobs = without directly competing for local jobs ประเด็นสำคัญคือคำในโจทย์ถูก paraphrase เป็นคำใน passage แต่ความหมายยังเหมือนเดิม จึงเลือก YES

Paraphrased Vocabulary: bring foreign income = earn money elsewhere but spend it locally; taking local jobs = competing for local jobs; answer signal = YES

Question 6: In every host city, digital nomads earn more than local residents.

Correct Answer: NOT GIVEN

Accepted Answers: NOT GIVEN

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Rise of Digital Nomads

Exact Portion: Cities with beaches, reliable internet and relatively low living costs can attract a sudden flow of higher-earning visitors.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ NOT GIVEN เพราะ passage มีข้อมูลใกล้เคียงว่า “Cities with beaches, reliable internet and relatively low living costs can attract a sudden flow of higher-earning visitors.” แต่ไม่ได้ยืนยันรายละเอียดเฉพาะที่โจทย์ถาม passage บอก higher-earning visitors ในบางเมือง แต่ไม่ได้บอกว่า every host city หรือเทียบกับ local residents ทุกที่ ดังนั้นไม่มีหลักฐานพอจะตอบ YES/NO หรือ TRUE/FALSE จึงเป็น NOT GIVEN

Paraphrased Vocabulary: every host city = no information; earn more than local residents = higher-earning visitors is not enough; answer signal = NOT GIVEN

Question 7: Companies may face legal or tax problems if employees work abroad without approval.

Correct Answer: YES

Accepted Answers: YES

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Rise of Digital Nomads

Exact Portion: If an employee works from another country without permission, the company may create tax, immigration or employment-law obligations without realising it.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ YES เพราะข้อความในโจทย์สอดคล้องกับหลักฐานใน passage: “If an employee works from another country without permission, the company may create tax, immigration or employment-law obligations without realising it.” without approval = without permission และ legal or tax problems = tax, immigration or employment-law obligations ประเด็นสำคัญคือคำในโจทย์ถูก paraphrase เป็นคำใน passage แต่ความหมายยังเหมือนเดิม จึงเลือก YES

Paraphrased Vocabulary: companies = company; legal or tax problems = tax, immigration or employment-law obligations; approval = permission; answer signal = YES

Question 8: Moving between places, and answer signal ....................
Correct Answer: technologies

Accepted Answers: technologies

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Rise of Digital Nomads

Exact Portion: someone who uses digital technologies to work remotely while living or travelling away from a fixed workplace

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ technologies เพราะ summary ใช้ถ้อยคำถอดความ ไม่ได้คัดลอกวลีจาก passage ตรงช่องว่าง — ช่องว่างต้องเติมคำจากหลักฐานใน passage: “someone who uses digital technologies to work remotely while living or travelling away from a fixed workplace” uses digital ____ to work remotely ตรงกับ digital technologies คำนี้พอดีกับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคและเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY


Paraphrased Vocabulary: between places and answer signal = someone who uses digital technologies to work remotely while living; answer signal = technologies

Question 9: Many countries, and legally visible ....................
Correct Answer: visas

Accepted Answers: visas

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Rise of Digital Nomads

Exact Portion: Governments have responded by creating special visa schemes for remote workers.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ visas เพราะ summary ใช้ถ้อยคำถอดความ ไม่ได้คัดลอกวลีจาก passage ตรงช่องว่าง — ช่องว่างต้องเติมคำจากหลักฐานใน passage: “Governments have responded by creating special visa schemes for remote workers.” created special ____ ตรงกับ special visa schemes คำตอบแบบ ONE WORD คือ visas คำนี้พอดีกับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคและเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY


Paraphrased Vocabulary: many countries and legally visible = governments have responded by creating special visa schemes for remote; answer signal = visas

Question 10: Applicants are often asked, and answer signal ....................
Correct Answer: income

Accepted Answers: income

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Rise of Digital Nomads

Exact Portion: Most schemes require applicants to show proof of income, health insurance and a contract with a foreign employer or clients outside the host country.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ income เพราะ summary ใช้ถ้อยคำถอดความ ไม่ได้คัดลอกวลีจาก passage ตรงช่องว่าง — ช่องว่างต้องเติมคำจากหลักฐานใน passage: “Most schemes require applicants to show proof of income, health insurance and a contract with a foreign employer or clients outside the host country.” provide evidence = show proof และ evidence of their ____ = proof of income คำนี้พอดีกับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคและเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY


Paraphrased Vocabulary: often asked and answer signal = most schemes require applicants to show proof of income health; answer signal = income

Question 11: Asked to provide, and answer signal ....................
Correct Answer: insurance

Accepted Answers: insurance

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Rise of Digital Nomads

Exact Portion: Most schemes require applicants to show proof of income, health insurance and a contract with a foreign employer or clients outside the host country.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ insurance เพราะ summary ใช้ถ้อยคำถอดความ ไม่ได้คัดลอกวลีจาก passage ตรงช่องว่าง — ช่องว่างต้องเติมคำจากหลักฐานใน passage: “Most schemes require applicants to show proof of income, health insurance and a contract with a foreign employer or clients outside the host country.” health ____ ตรงกับ health insurance คำนี้พอดีกับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคและเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY


Paraphrased Vocabulary: to provide and answer signal = most schemes require applicants to show proof of income health; answer signal = insurance

Question 12: Host economies, and answer signal ....................
Correct Answer: spending

Accepted Answers: spending

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Rise of Digital Nomads

Exact Portion: Cafes, coworking spaces and apartment owners may welcome the spending

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ spending เพราะ summary ใช้ถ้อยคำถอดความ ไม่ได้คัดลอกวลีจาก passage ตรงช่องว่าง — ช่องว่างต้องเติมคำจากหลักฐานใน passage: “Cafes, coworking spaces and apartment owners may welcome the spending” benefit from nomads' local ____ ตรงกับ may welcome the spending คำนี้พอดีกับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคและเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY


Paraphrased Vocabulary: host economies and answer signal = cafes coworking spaces and apartment owners may welcome the spending; answer signal = spending

Question 13: Worry, and answer signal ....................
Correct Answer: rents

Accepted Answers: rents

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Rise of Digital Nomads

Exact Portion: residents may see rents rise faster than wages

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ rents เพราะ summary ใช้ถ้อยคำถอดความ ไม่ได้คัดลอกวลีจาก passage ตรงช่องว่าง — ช่องว่างต้องเติมคำจากหลักฐานใน passage: “residents may see rents rise faster than wages” housing ____ will increase ตรงกับ rents rise คำนี้พอดีกับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคและเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY


Paraphrased Vocabulary: worry and answer signal = residents may see rents rise faster than wages; answer signal = rents
